description.en: A dense white loaf raised entirely by wild bacteria captured in a milk-and-cornmeal starter, not by yeast at all. Devised in isolated Appalachian households that had no reliable yeast, it carries a faint, distinctive tang some bakers describe as cheesy.
description.de: Ein dichtes weißes Brot, das ganz von wilden Bakterien in einem Milch-Maismehl-Ansatz gelockert wird, nicht von Hefe. Entstanden in abgelegenen Appalachen-Haushalten ohne verlässliche Hefe, mit einer leichten, unverkennbaren Note, die manche als käsig beschreiben.
description.es: Un pan blanco denso, leudado por completo con bacterias silvestres capturadas en un fermento de leche y maíz, sin nada de levadura. Ideado en hogares aislados de los Apalaches sin levadura fiable, tiene un toque distintivo que algunos describen como a queso.
description.fr: Un pain blanc dense, entièrement levé par des bactéries sauvages captées dans un ferment de lait et de semoule de maïs, sans aucune levure. Inventé dans des foyers isolés des Appalaches sans levure fiable, il garde une pointe distinctive que certains disent fromagée.

origin.note: Devised by isolated pioneer households in the late 1700s-1800s with no reliable yeast. Wild bacteria, not yeast or a classic sourdough culture, do the leavening; classified here as sourdough since the schema has no bacterial-ferment category.

ingredients.note: for the starter; ideally stone-ground, not degerminated
split between the starter and the flour sponge, to feed the wild bacterial culture
hot, about 49-54°C, for the flour sponge
in the sponge, to tame its sourness
split between the sponge and the final dough

keeping.note: Keeps like an ordinary white sandwich loaf; the real challenge is coaxing the wild starter to bubble, not keeping the baked bread fresh.

method.title: Start the culture
method.summary: Combine milk, cornmeal and a little sugar in a jar; ferment 8-12 hours at a warm 32-38°C until visibly bubbly and pleasantly sour.

method.title: Build the sponge
method.summary: Stir the bubbly starter into hot water with salt, baking soda, sugar and flour; ferment another 2-4 hours at the same warm temperature until doubled and foaming.

method.title: Mix the final dough
method.summary: Work in softened butter, more salt and enough flour to form a soft, kneadable dough.

method.title: Knead briefly
method.summary: Knead just until smooth and no longer sticky; little gluten development is needed since the rise is slow and gentle.

method.title: Pan and proof
method.summary: Shape into a loaf pan and proof, still at a warm 32-38°C, up to 4 hours until it crowns just above the rim.

method.title: Bake
method.summary: Bake at 175°C for 35-40 minutes until deep golden and an internal temperature of about 90-93°C.

method.title: Cool
method.summary: Cool completely before slicing; the crumb is dense, fine and faintly tangy.
